disambiguation.rationale: In the verse context, this is an accusative plural of duration of time ('for twenty-five years').
It functions as a temporal indeclinable adverb meaning 'since' or 'from the time when'.

This is the past participle used in a predicate position modifying the speaker, agreeing in gender/number with the underlying subject ('since I went forth' or 'since I have been ordained'). Note: Although the text says 'pabbajito' (masculine), in the Therīgāthā variant 'pabbajitā' is feminine, but here it matches the past participle 'pabbajito'.
It is the nominative singular of the first-person pronoun 'I'.
It is the deconstructed form 'accharāsaṅghātamattaṃ + api' (even for a finger snap).

translation_parsed_response.translation: It is twenty-five years since I went forth, yet I have not attained even a moment's peace of mind.
translation_parsed_response.literal_translation: Twenty-five years [it is], since gone forth I [am], even a finger-snap-measure, peace of mind I did not reach.
